rulings.Case Title: ANDREI URAZOV VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 1. Petition Of Writ Of Mandate Continued to May 26, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. Real Party in Interest to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copy of the opposition and objections no later than May 16, 2016 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Application To Appoint A Receiver Pursuant To Ccp 564 And 1281.8
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 2. PETITIONER PAOLO MANCINI's Application To Appoint A Receiver Pursuant To Ccp 564 And 1281.8 Hearing Required to address the status of resolution of the appointment of receiver. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SALEM F MUFARREH VS. BERNARD DAVID WALTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The First Cause Of Action In The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANTS BERNARD WALTER and RONAN ERICKSON's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On The First Cause Of Action In The Complaint DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact including but not limited to whether Defendant is deprived of the use of its land completely; the scope of the easement; whether use was open and notorious; whether Plaintiff can store the garbage bins elsewhere. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA L. MASTER VS. TAN ZI MING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 4. PLAINTIFFS MARIA MASTER and CONSTATINA MASTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED. Defendant failed to file and serve its Substantive Opposition pursuant to the Court Order dated April 29, 2016. Thus, Plaintiff prevails on the First cause of Action (quiet title), Second cause of action (declaratory relief), Third cause of action (Trespass), Fourth cause of action (nuisance) and Ninth cause of action (injunctive relief) and is entitled to damages and injunctive relief on each.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD L. WESTON VS. MIMI LAY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And For The Issuance Of An Amended Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 5. Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs And For The Issuance Of An Amended Judgment Continued to June 10, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 501 on Court's own motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BROOKS ET AL VS. FEI YU LI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT FEI LI's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint DENIED. Defendant failed to meet his burden to show that the sections that are sought to be stricken are "irrelevant, false or improper," or "not drawn in conformity with the laws of the state, court rule or order of the court." (CCP § 436.) P sufficiently pleads facts that could lead a finder of fact to determine that Defendant's conduct was "despicable," and not mere carelessness or ignorance. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BROOKS ET AL VS. FEI YU LI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 6. DEFENDANT FEI LI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Plaintiff's negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is not prohibited under California law. (See Cruz v. Ayromloo (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1273.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to sustain its cause of action because Plaintiff alleges that Plaintiff and Defendant had a preexisting relationship and Defendant's conduct caused Plaintiff's alleged severe emotional distress. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JACKSON TOWER ASSOCIATES, L.P., VS. CHRISTOPHER COMPAGNO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, May 13, 2016, line 7. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER COMPAGNO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED and case dismissed until such time as Plaintiff first brings his action before the S.F. Rent Board. (See DeLaura v. Beckett (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 542.) = (501/REQ)
